title: TrailGenic Seven Day Aftereffect Dataset
description: Shell dataset module for TrailGenic physiology adaptation science modeling extended endurance adaptation following high-duration aerobic stimulus.

records.mechanism: Extended mitochondrial signaling following prolonged endurance stimulus
physiological_response: increased AMPK signaling following prolonged energy stress
adaptation_outcomes: improved endurance efficiency in subsequent sessions

supporting_science.finding: Exercise activates molecular signaling pathways including AMPK and PGC-1alpha which regulate mitochondrial biogenesis.
Endurance exercise induces transcriptional responses that promote mitochondrial adaptation and oxidative metabolism.

records.mechanism: Elevated fat oxidation capacity following glycogen depletion
physiological_response: increased reliance on fatty acid oxidation
increased mitochondrial fatty acid oxidation capacity
adaptation_outcomes: reduced dependence on glycogen during submaximal exercise

supporting_science.finding: Endurance training enhances skeletal muscle oxidative capacity and fat utilization during prolonged exercise.
Fat oxidation during endurance exercise increases with training status and aerobic adaptation.

physiological_response: prolonged metabolic signaling following endurance activity
increased protein synthesis related to mitochondrial enzymes
enhanced substrate transport into muscle cells
adaptation_outcomes: improved metabolic efficiency in subsequent training sessions
greater endurance stability across weekly training cycles

supporting_science.finding: Exercise triggers molecular responses that persist post-exercise and influence adaptation to endurance training.
Training adaptations occur through cumulative molecular signaling responses to repeated endurance exercise.
